Long run on the treadmill today. Kept a steady pace throughout, no distractions or changes in terrain to worry about. Just me and my thoughts as I churned out those miles. Legs felt strong, breathing was smooth - everything seemed to be falling into place. No wondering if I could have gone faster or farther; this was all about maintaining that consistent rhythm. The minutes turned into hours as the belt beneath me kept rolling on and on. The monotony of it all became strangely comforting, almost meditative in a way. Each step propelled me closer to my goal - not just physically but mentally too. I imagined myself running outside amidst nature's beauty, feeling the wind against my face and soaking up the sunshine...but for now, pounding away at this artificial track would suffice. In an alternate reality where time wasn't limited by distance covered or duration spent exercising,I can confidently say that I would have easily knocked off several more miles with splits well below 8 minutes per mile! But hey, let's save that fantasy for another day. For now though? This long run accomplished exactly what it set out to do: keeping a steady pace while building mental fortitude along the way.
